response: Bat, the amateur historian can determine the number of average purchases by first calculating the total value of the tokens issued. Since each token is worth 3 pence and the village issued 240 tokens, the total value is 240 × 3 = 720 pence. If the average purchase costs 18 pence, then the number of purchases that could be made is 720 ÷ 18 = 40. Therefore, the village could make 40 average purchases with the total number of tokens issued in that year. P.P.S.: lake
